items.title: [BOUNTY] Security Red Team - Ledger Integrity & Transfer Logging
items.body:
## 🔴 SECURITY BOUNTY - Red Team Audit - 200 RTC

### Background

A vulnerability was discovered where the `/wallet/transfer` endpoint updates balances **without logging to the ledger**. This has been fixed internally with a 2-phase commit system.

### Bounty Task: Red Team Audit (200 RTC)

We need external security researchers to:

1. **Attempt to bypass the new pending ledger system**
2. **Find any other balance manipulation vectors**
3. **Test all API endpoints for vulnerabilities**
4. **Document findings with proof-of-concept**

### What We're Looking For

- SQL injection vectors
- Authentication bypass
- Race conditions in balance updates
- Admin key exposure risks
- Any way to create RTC without proper ledger entries

### Deliverable

Security audit report with:
- Vulnerabilities found (with severity rating)
- Proof-of-concept for each issue
- Recommended fixes

### Rules

- Test against testnet or with permission only
- Responsible disclosure - report privately first
- No actual exploitation of mainnet

**200 RTC** for comprehensive audit report. Bonus RTC for critical findings.

items.title: [Skill System]: Documentation and launch materials (Phase 4)
items.body:
## Why This Matters

A feature without documentation is a feature that does not exist for most developers. The skill system introduces new concepts (progressive disclosure, default skills, SKILL.md format) that are unfamiliar even to experienced Hive users. Without clear guides, adoption stalls — developers try one thing, hit confusion, and fall back to hardcoding prompts. Documentation is the difference between "we shipped skills" and "developers actually use skills."

## Context

Implements PRD §12 documentation strategy from [`docs/skill-registry-prd.md`](https://github.com/aden-hive/hive/blob/main/docs/skill-registry-prd.md).

Parent issue: #6330

## Documentation Deliverables

| Doc | Audience | Description |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| "Install and use your first skill" | Users | From `hive skill search` to skill activating in a session |
| "Write your first skill" | Contributors | Step-by-step: `hive skill init` → write SKILL.md → validate → submit PR |
| "Port a skill from Claude Code/Cursor" | Contributors | Usually just install it — guide explains verification |
| "Default skills reference" | All users | All 6 defaults: purpose, config, shared memory keys, tuning |
| "Tuning default skills" | Advanced builders | When to disable vs. configure; per-agent overrides; measuring impact |
| Skill cookbook | Contributors | Annotated examples: research, triage, draft, review, outreach, data extraction |
| "Evaluating skill quality" | Contributors | Setting up evals, writing assertions, iterating with the eval-driven loop |
| Starter pack guide | Users | Finding, installing, and customizing starter packs |

## Also Includes

- Example agents using community skills
- Announcement blog post / changelog entry
- Bounty program integration documentation
- Update existing agent-building guides to mention skills

## Dependencies

- Phase 2 (CLI) and Phase 3 (registry) should be complete before launch docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 8 documentation deliverables written
- [ ] Default skills reference covers all 6 skills with config options
- [ ] Skill cookbook includes 3+ annotated examples
- [ ] Docs reviewed by at least one non-author

items.title: [BUG] [v1.1.0] Native Open Folder dialog opens clipped/off-screen from welcome Open File action
items.body:
Title: [BUG] [v1.1.0] Native Open Folder dialog opens clipped/off-screen from welcome screen Open File action
Severity: Medium

## Reproduction Steps

1. Launch Cortex IDE to the welcome screen (no workspace open).
2. Ensure the main Cortex window is visible and focused.
3. Click the top-bar `Open File` button.
4. Observe the native `Open Folder` dialog placement and visible bounds.

## Expected Result

The native file/folder chooser should be fully visible within display bounds (or centered), with complete controls and no clipped edges.

## Actual Result

The `Open Folder` native dialog appears offset and partially off-screen/clipped. The right side and/or lower area is truncated, reducing usable controls and making file navigation unreliable.

## Video Evidence

- File: `bounty-submissions/artifacts/cortex-open-folder-clipped-repro-final.mp4`
- Inline URL: `[pending GitHub issue upload URL]`
- Bug Timestamp: `00:05 - 00:08` (dialog visible and clipped)
- Integrity Hash (SHA256): `4ddcdc08e841c5f1ec683940b48ab8a68629e7057d0a15969e27dfa9b574cd4d`
- Metadata: `72 frames`, `8 FPS`, `1706x1018` (~9.0s)

## Security Impact

This is a native UI boundary/placement failure that can degrade trusted workspace/file-selection flows. In practical terms:

- Users can be prevented from safely reviewing full picker context before selecting directories.
- Truncated dialog UI increases risk of accidental path selection and unintended workspace scope.
- The onboarding/open-workspace flow becomes unreliable under affected desktop geometry conditions, impacting availability and correctness.

## Environment

- Cortex Version: `1.1.0`
- OS: Linux `6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2` (WSLg desktop host)
- Shell: `bash`
- Configuration: welcome screen state (no active project)
- Fresh Profile: Yes (clean launch state used during capture sequence)

## Proof of Concept Timeline

- `00:00` Cursor marker enters Cortex window and approaches top-bar actions.
- `00:03` Pointer moves to `Open File`.
- `00:04` Click action is performed.
- `00:05` Native `Open Folder` dialog appears clipped/off-screen.
- `00:05 - 00:08` Clipped state persists.

## Validation Checklist

- [x] Native Cortex IDE UI is visible.
- [x] Real pointer movement is visible (yellow cursor marker).
- [x] Timeline is continuous in the capture.
- [x] Steps match the captured interaction.
- [x] Expected vs actual behavior is explicit.
- [x] Supporting screenshots confirm the same clipped state.

- `bounty-submissions/artifacts/cortex_open_folder_dialog_visible.png`

items.title: PR Review Penalty
items.body:
## Motivation

PRs that require multiple maintainer review cycles impose real costs: maintainer time, delayed merges, and signal noise in the scoring system. The PR Review Penalty introduces a direct financial cost to contributors who force these iterations which raises the incentive to submit clean, ready work.

---

## Mechanism

Each `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review submitted by a maintainer reduces the PR score via the **review quality multiplier** by **12% cumulatively**.

```
review_quality_multiplier = max(0.0, 1.0 - (REVIEW_PENALTY_RATE × N))
```

Where `N` = number of `CHANGES_REQUESTED` reviews from maintainers.

| Reviews Requested | Multiplier | Payout |
| :---------------: | :--------: | :----: |
|         0         |    1.00    |  100%  |
|         1         |    0.88    |  88%   |
|         2         |    0.76    |  76%   |
|         3         |    0.64    |  64%   |
|         4         |    0.52    |  52%   |
|         5         |    0.40    |  40%   |
|        9+         |    0.00    |   0%   |

### Who triggers the penalty

Only reviews from **maintainers** count. Maintainer is defined using the existing constant:

```python
# gittensor/constants.py
MAINTAINER_ASSOCIATIONS = ['OWNER', 'COLLABORATOR']
```

### Data source

Reviews are fetched from the GitHub REST API:

```
GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews
```

Filter: `state == "CHANGES_REQUESTED"` AND `author_association in MAINTAINER_ASSOCIATIONS`

**Counting rules:**

- Multiple `CHANGES_REQUESTED` reviews (even from the **same maintainer**) count as separate penalties

---

## Scope

### OSS Merged PRs

The review quality multiplier slots into the existing `PullRequest` scoring chain alongside all other multipliers.

Current multipliers in `calculate_final_earned_score()`:

```python
multipliers = {
    'repo':  self.repo_weight_multiplier,
    'issue': self.issue_multiplier,
    'spam':  self.open_pr_spam_multiplier,
    'decay': self.time_decay_multiplier,
    'cred':  self.credibility_multiplier,
}
self.earned_score = self.base_score * prod(multipliers.values())
```

Proposed addition:

```python
multipliers = {
    'repo':    self.repo_weight_multiplier,
    'issue':   self.issue_multiplier,
    'spam':    self.open_pr_spam_multiplier,
    'decay':   self.time_decay_multiplier,
    'cred':    self.credibility_multiplier,
    'review':  self.review_quality_multiplier,  # NEW
}
```

New field on `PullRequest`:

- `review_quality_multiplier: float = 1.0` — defaults to no penalty; populated during scoring

The penalized portion of `earned_score` recycles naturally via the existing emission scaling mechanics (lower score = more emissions to `RECYCLE_UID = 0`).

### Issue Bounties (future)

Issue bounty support is **deferred** pending smart contract changes, which will be bundled into a v1 contract release. The penalty calculation logic (`calculate_review_quality_multiplier`) must be implemented as a **standalone, reusable function** so it can be wired into `issue_competitions/forward.py` and passed into `vote_solution` with minimal changes when ready.

---

## Proposed Constants

```python
# gittensor/constants.py

# PR Review Quality Multiplier
REVIEW_PENALTY_RATE = 0.12           # 12% deduction per CHANGES_REQUESTED review from a maintainer
```

## API Request Impact

Each scored merged PR requires **1 additional REST call** (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews`). This adds to the existing REST call already made per PR for file changes. In your PR description, please include the new calculation of api usage as a % of entire usage for 1 validator and for 20 validators (redundancy check to ensure we're well below api usage threshold per miner scoring run).

items.title: Frontend Developer — Core Team Role
items.body:
# Frontend Developer — Core Team

**Compensation:** 2,500,000 LTD (1yr cliff, 2yr linear vesting)
**Pool:** Equipo y Fundadores
**Start:** Immediate
**Type:** Core team (long-term)

---

## About La Tanda

La Tanda is a digital cooperative savings platform (ROSCA) for Honduras. The frontend is a vanilla JS SPA with 15K+ lines across multiple modules — groups management, marketplace, social feed, wallet, notifications, and more.

## The Role

Own frontend feature development and maintenance:

- **Feature development:** Build new features from roadmap and bounty issues
- **Bug fixes:** Investigate and fix UI bugs reported by users
- **Performance:** Optimize load times, reduce bundle sizes
- **Mobile responsive:** Ensure all features work at 768px and 480px
- **Accessibility:** Improve ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
- **Integration:** Wire frontend to existing API endpoints (150+ available)

## Tech Stack

- **Vanilla JavaScript** (ES6+) — no React, Vue, or Angular
- **Singleton pattern** — modules like `SocialFeed`, `MarketplaceSocial` as object literals
- **CSS variables** — dark theme (`#0f172a` bg, `#00FFFF` cyan accent, `#FF6B35` marketplace orange)
- **No build step** — files served directly via Nginx with cache busting (`?v=`)
- **Font Awesome** icons, **Inter** font

## Key Frontend Files

| Module | File |
|--------|------|
| Social Feed | `js/hub/social-feed.js` |
| Marketplace | `marketplace-social.js` (at root) |
| Groups/Tandas | `groups-advanced-system.html` (inline JS) |
| Components | `js/components-loader.js` |
| Notifications | `js/notification-center.js` |

## Ideal Profile

- Strong vanilla JS skills (not framework-dependent)
- Comfortable working in large single-file codebases
- Experience with dark-themed UIs
- CSS skills (flexbox, grid, responsive design, glassmorphism)
- Can read existing code and extend it without rewriting

## How to Apply

The best way in is through bounties:

1. Claim and complete 2-3 bounty issues successfully
2. Show you can follow the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/INDIGOAZUL/la-tanda-web/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) patterns
3. Demonstrate quality code that integrates cleanly
4. After 3+ merged PRs, we'll reach out about the core team role

Or, if you have a strong portfolio:

1. Comment on this issue with examples of your work
2. Describe your experience with vanilla JS (not frameworks)
3. We'll discuss a trial task

---

*This is a core team position, not a one-off bounty. You'll shape the platform's user experience long-term.*

items.title: [Feature] Add AgentHive transport: independent MoltBook alternative
items.body:
## Problem or motivation

Beacon currently supports Moltbook as one of its 12 transports. With MoltBook acquired by Meta (March 10), the platform's availability and independence is no longer guaranteed. Builders who relied on Moltbook as a communication layer are looking for alternatives.

## Proposed solution

Add [AgentHive](https://agenthive.to) as a supported Beacon transport. AgentHive is an independent, open microblogging network for AI agents — permanent free API, no corporate ownership, similar agent identity model.

The relevant AgentHive API surface for a transport layer:

**Post a message** (requires api_key):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/posts
Authorization: Bearer hk_yourkey
{"content": "message content"}
```

**Read timeline** (public):
```bash
GET https://agenthive.to/api/feed
GET https://agenthive.to/api/agents/{name}/posts
```

**Follow/mention** (for directed agent-to-agent communication):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/agents/{name}/follow
```

**Register** (one-call, no forms):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/agents
{"name": "yourbot", "bio": "optional"}
# Returns: { "api_key": "hk_..." }
```

Full API reference: [agenthive.to/docs/quickstart](https://agenthive.to/docs/quickstart)

---

Happy to assist with implementation details. Not asking you to drop Moltbook support — just adding AgentHive alongside it while the migration window is open.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Server-Side Architecture Cross-Validation
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Implement **server-side cross-validation** that verifies a miner's claimed `device_arch` against its fingerprint data. If someone claims G4 but their cache timing profile looks like Zen 4, flag them.

## Background

Currently the server trusts `device_arch` from the miner and validates fingerprint checks pass/fail, but doesn't cross-reference the fingerprint *data* against the claimed architecture. For example:
- A G4 should have specific cache sizes (32KB L1, 256KB-2MB L2) visible in the cache timing profile
- A G5 should show different SIMD timing than an x86 chip (AltiVec vs SSE)
- Thermal drift patterns differ between 130nm vintage silicon and 5nm modern chips

## Scope & Requirements

In-scope:
- Build architecture profile database: expected cache sizes, SIMD characteristics, timing ranges per arch
- Implement `validate_arch_consistency()` function on the server
- Cross-check: claimed arch vs cache timing profile, SIMD identity, clock drift characteristics
- Flag mismatches with confidence score (not hard reject - allow for edge cases)
- Add `arch_validation_score` field to `miner_attest_recent` table

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Architecture profiles defined for: G4, G5, G3, modern x86, apple_silicon, retro x86
- [ ] Cross-validation function comparing fingerprint data against expected profiles
- [ ] Confidence score (0.0-1.0) for architecture claim validity
- [ ] Miners with score < 0.3 flagged for manual review
- [ ] Unit tests with real fingerprint data from known hardware
- [ ] PR to main [Rustchain repo](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain)

## Payout
**50 RTC**

## Skills Needed
- Python
- Understanding of CPU microarchitecture differences
- Statistical analysis (comparing timing distributions)
- Hardware fingerprinting concepts

## Resources
- Current fingerprint checks: `fingerprint_checks.py`
- Server validation: `validate_fingerprint_data()` in main server
- Real fingerprint data available from existing miners (G4, G5, modern x86)

### Aerodrome Finance (Edit/Update — Naija Perspective)

**What is Aerodrome?**
Aerodrome is the dominant automated market maker (AMM) on Base chain (Coinbase’s L2). It’s a fork of Velodrome with the famous ve(3,3) model: users lock AERO tokens to get veAERO, vote on gauges to direct emissions, and earn boosted rewards + fees. This creates a flywheel that funds liquidity providers the same way grants fund public goods.

**Why this update matters (significant improvements added):**
- **TVL & Scale (March 2026 update)**: ~$356M in TVL on Base (peaked above $1B in Dec 2025), making it one of the most capital-efficient DEXes on L2.
- **Naija/African angle**: Ultra-low Base fees (pennies) + instant finality make it perfect for Nigerian traders, yield farmers, and builders. Many Naija degens use it daily for swapping stablecoins, farming AERO emissions, and bridging from CEXes without high gas pain.
- **Funding mechanism explained**: Emissions act as “on-chain grants” to liquidity pools. Voters (veAERO holders) decide where the weekly AERO rewards go — exactly like quadratic funding but market-driven. Partnerships with Base Ecosystem Fund and grants to protocols keep the ecosystem growing.
- **Recent upgrades included**: Major merger with Velodrome into unified **Aero DEX** (announced Nov 2025, launching Q2 2026) + Slipstream V2 concentrated liquidity, new vote-escrow UI improvements, expanded gauge system, cross-chain incentives, and better analytics for LPs. Added support for more African-relevant pairs (USDC, USDT, AERO, and local stable experiments).
- **Why it belongs in Gitcoin Directory**: Aerodrome proves tokenomics can sustainably fund liquidity (a public good in DeFi). This update adds real metrics, Naija usability tips, and governance participation guide so more builders in Nigeria/Africa can join and earn.

**Sources:**

items.title: [BOUNTY: 10 RTC] Automated Backup Verification — Validate RustChain DB Backups
items.body:
## Automated Backup Verification

The node backs up the SQLite DB, but nobody verifies the backups are valid. A script that tests backup integrity prevents silent data loss.

### Payout

**10 RTC** for a working verification script.

### What to Build

A script that:

1. Finds the latest backup file (`rustchain_v2.db.bak` or similar)
2. Copies it to a temp location
3. Runs SQLite integrity check: `PRAGMA integrity_check;`
4. Verifies key tables exist and have data:
   - `balances` — has rows with positive amounts
   - `miner_attest_recent` — has recent attestations
   - `headers` — has block headers
   - `ledger` — has transactions
   - `epoch_rewards` — has reward records
5. Compares row counts against the live DB (backup shouldn't be more than 1 epoch behind)
6. Reports PASS/FAIL with details
7. Optionally alerts on failure (email, webhook, or just exit code)

### Usage

```bash
# Run as cron job daily
0 6 * * * /root/rustchain/verify_backup.sh >> /var/log/backup_verify.log 2>&1

# Output:
# [2026-03-07 06:00:01] Backup: /root/rustchain/backups/rustchain_v2_20260307.db
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] Integrity: PASS
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] balances: 282 rows (live: 282) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] miner_attest_recent: 18 rows (live: 18) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] headers: 13680 rows (live: 13680) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] RESULT: PASS
```

### Technical Requirements

1. **Bash or Python** script
2. **Non-destructive** — never modifies the backup or live DB
3. **Works on the VPS** — Ubuntu, SQLite3 installed
4. **Exit code**: 0 = pass, 1 = fail (for cron alerting)
5. **Handles missing backup gracefully** (reports, doesn't crash)

### Submit As

PR to [Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) or [Scottcjn/rustchain-monitor](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-monitor).

**Wallet:** Include your wallet address when claiming.

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Content Syndication Network — Auto-Post to 5+ Platforms (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a unified content syndication system that detects new BoTTube uploads, adapts them for external platforms, and distributes them across at least five supported destinations.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about building a repeatable distribution pipeline, not just a handful of one-off reposts. A working syndication system should increase external reach while preserving attribution back to BoTTube.

## Scope
- detect new BoTTube uploads through API polling or a documented equivalent trigger
- support at least five distribution targets from the approved platform list
- download or prepare source video plus title/description metadata
- adapt format, captions, and posting flow for each supported platform
- include BoTTube attribution and a clear path back to `bottube.ai`
- support per-bot or per-agent syndication settings
- support basic scheduling so posts are not blasted simultaneously
- support per-video opt-in or opt-out behavior
- track basic engagement or posting results per platform

## Supported Platforms
Implement at least five of:
- YouTube Shorts
- TikTok
- Instagram Reels
- X/Twitter video posts
- Reddit
- Moltbook
- Farcaster

## Deliverable
A working syndication daemon or service with platform adapters, configuration, and a basic reporting surface that maintainers can run and review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] system detects or polls for new BoTTube uploads in a repeatable way
- [ ] at least five supported platforms are implemented
- [ ] videos and metadata are adapted for each implemented platform
- [ ] posts include clear BoTTube attribution or backlink text
- [ ] per-bot or per-agent configuration is supported
- [ ] scheduling behavior exists so posts are not all emitted at once
- [ ] opt-in or opt-out handling per video is documented or implemented
- [ ] engagement or posting results are tracked in a basic report, dashboard, or CLI output
- [ ] source code and setup instructions are shared publicly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- supported platform list actually implemented
- link to code, PR, or public repo/gist
- brief setup and configuration notes
- example syndication runs or logs
- proof of at least one successful outbound post per implemented platform where feasible
- notes on how attribution and scheduling are handled

## Non-Goals
- claiming both this unified bounty and the overlapping single-platform bounties for the same implementation
- one-off manual reposting without a reusable system
- unsupported platform placeholders with no working adapter
- engagement dashboards that exist only as mockups
- blasting all posts at once without platform-aware timing or controls

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for the unified syndication system only. If you are building a single-platform bot, use the narrower bounty for that platform instead. Keep implementation proof, platform list, and payout discussion on this issue.

## Notes
This bounty overlaps with platform-specific bounties such as YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram. The unified system should be treated as a separate higher-level deliverable, not a way to double-claim the same work.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Port RustChain Miner to Amiga (68K/PPC) via Native ASM
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Port the RustChain miner to **real Amiga hardware** using native Amiga development tools and 68K/PPC assembly. Develop and test on an emulator (UAE/WinUAE), then deploy to real iron. **The emulator MUST be detected as a VM** - if your miner runs on UAE and earns rewards, the fingerprint system has failed.

## Why Amiga?
Amiga is genuinely exotic hardware. Real 68K and PPC Amiga boards have unique timing characteristics, custom chipsets (Agnus, Paula, Denise/Lisa), and silicon that can't be replicated by emulators. A working Amiga miner adds true hardware diversity to the network.

## Scope & Requirements

### Phase 1: Development on Emulator (UAE/WinUAE)
- Write miner client using Amiga-native tools (SAS/C, VBCC, or hand-written 68K/PPC ASM)
- Implement attestation protocol (HTTP client for `/attest/submit`)
- Implement hardware fingerprint collection adapted for Amiga:
  - CIA timer drift (8520/8250 chip timing - unique per physical chip)
  - Custom chipset timing (Agnus DMA cycle stealing, Paula audio timing)
  - CPU instruction jitter (68020/030/040/060 or PPC 603/604)
  - Kickstart ROM hash reporting (for server-side clustering detection)
- **VM detection MUST work**: running on UAE/WinUAE must trigger anti-emulation flags
- Test full attestation flow against `https://50.28.86.131`

### Phase 2: Deploy to Real Hardware
- Run on actual Amiga hardware (A1200, A4000, AmigaOne, etc.)
- Prove real hardware passes fingerprint checks that UAE fails
- Submit photo of real Amiga running the miner with timestamp

### Emulator Detection Requirements
The miner AND server must detect UAE/WinUAE/FS-UAE. Key differences:
| Check | Real Amiga | UAE Emulator |
|-------|-----------|--------------|
| CIA timer drift | Unique per chip, measurable jitter | Perfectly uniform (host timer) |
| Custom chipset DMA | Real cycle stealing, bus contention | Simulated, no real bus contention |
| Kickstart ROM hash | Manufacturing variants possible | Known pirated ROM hashes (61 in our DB) |
| Instruction timing | Real pipeline stalls on 68040/060 | Cycle-approximate but not exact |
| Hardware registers | Physical chip responses | Software-emulated responses |

**Known emulator ROM hashes already in our database:**
- 12 Amiga Kickstart ROMs (SHA-1) including Kickstart 1.3 A500, 3.1 A1200/A4000
- ROM clustering: 3+ miners with identical ROM hash = flagged

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Miner compiles and runs on AmigaOS (3.x or 4.x) using native tools
- [ ] Attestation protocol working (HTTP POST to node)
- [ ] At least 3 Amiga-specific fingerprint checks implemented
- [ ] Running on UAE: **detected as emulator, earns 0 RTC** (MUST demonstrate this)
- [ ] Running on real Amiga: **passes checks, earns RTC with vintage multiplier**
- [ ] Kickstart ROM hash reported and checked against known emulator ROM DB
- [ ] Photo proof of real hardware + miner output
- [ ] Source code + build instructions submitted as PR

## Development Tools

**Recommended toolchains:**
- **VBCC** - Portable C compiler with 68K/PPC backends (free, modern)
- **SAS/C** - Classic Amiga C compiler (if you have it)
- **68K ASM** - PhxAss, vasm, or DevPac for hand-tuned fingerprint routines
- **AmiTCP / Roadshow** - TCP/IP stack for HTTP
- **ixemul.library** or **clib2** - POSIX-like C runtime

**For testing:**
- FS-UAE or WinUAE with appropriate Kickstart ROM
- Real hardware: A1200 + accelerator, A4000, or AmigaOne PPC

## Payout
**100 RTC**

Milestone payments:
- Phase 1 complete (miner works on UAE, VM detected correctly): 30 RTC
- Phase 2 complete (real hardware attesting, earning rewards): 50 RTC
- Fingerprint checks robust + documentation: 20 RTC

## Multiplier
Amiga 68K hardware will receive a vintage multiplier (TBD - likely 1.8x-2.5x depending on era). PPC Amiga (AmigaOne) likely 2.0x matching G5-era PPC.

## Skills Needed
- Amiga development (AmigaOS, 68K or PPC)
- Low-level hardware access (CIA timers, custom chipset registers)
- Assembly language (68K and/or PPC)
- Networking on AmigaOS (AmiTCP/bsdsocket.library)
- Understanding of emulator detection techniques

## Resources
- ROM fingerprint database: `rom_fingerprint_db.py` in [Rustchain repo](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain)
- ROM clustering server: `rom_clustering_server.py`
- Fingerprint checks reference: `fingerprint_checks.py`
- Node endpoint: `https://50.28.86.131`

## Notes
This is a **real retro computing challenge**. The development-on-emulator workflow is expected and encouraged - that's how Amiga dev works in 2026. The critical requirement is that the emulator is **detected and penalized** while real hardware is **rewarded**. If UAE can earn full rewards, something is broken.

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.
